title: standalone-verify failed to verify image signature
body:
I'm trying to verify the image signature, but it failed. I suspect the manifest is not the right one.
How to get the image's manifest file and what does `skopeo manifest-digest` used for?

```
[root@fedora ~]# skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false --remove-signatures docker-daemon:docker.io/busybox:latest docker://localhost:5000/skopeo/busybox:latest
Copying blob sha256:0271b8eebde3fa9a6126b1f2335e170f902731ab4942f9f1914e77016540c7bb
 1.28 MB / 1.28 MB [========================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:6ad733544a6317992a6fac4eb19fe1df577d4dec7529efec28a5bd0edad0fd30
 0 B / 1.46 KB [------------------------------------------------------------] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@fedora ~]# skopeo standalone-sign /tmp/skopeo/dir/manifest.json localhost:5000/skopeo/busybox:latest 82F1726CDE5082EB3B476AE699F7803D44F13569 -o busybox.signature
[root@fedora ~]# skopeo standalone-verify /tmp/skopeo/dir/manifest.json localhost:5000/skopeo/busybox:latest 82F1726CDE5082EB3B476AE699F7803D44F13569  busybox.signature
FATA[0000] Error verifying signature: Signature by 77327C08797C1E866C70EBBAEA54242AC9227639 does not match expected fingerprint 82F1726CDE5082EB3B476AE699F7803D44F13569 
```

comments.body:
> `FATA[0000] Error verifying signature: Signature by 77327C08797C1E866C70EBBAEA54242AC9227639 does not match expected fingerprint 82F1726CDE5082EB3B476AE699F7803D44F13569 `

This has nothing to do with the manifest, but with the key lookup: the key used to create the signature does not match the fingerprint on the `standalone-verify` command line. Are the two lines really recording two _immediately_ following commands? Can you reproduce the `standalone-sign`/`standalone-verify` command pair and get the same failure?

If you can, the output of `gpg --list-packets < busybox.signature`, and the output of `GPGME_DEBUG=9 skopeo standalone-sign…` could be useful. (Make sure to scrub the latter one of passphrase, if any.)

```
[root@fedora ~]# GPGME_DEBUG=9 skopeo standalone-sign busybox.manifest localhost:5000/skopeo/busybox:latest 82F1726CDE5082EB3B476AE699F7803D44F13569 -o busybox.signature
```

If I am reading this correctly, you have a primary key with fingerprint 82F1726CDE5082EB3B476AE699F7803D44F13569 , and a _subkey_ with fingerprint 77327C08797C1E866C70EBBAEA54242AC9227639 , and the signing capability enabled for that subkey.  So, the signature is created with the subkey, and the fingerprint which needs to match is the fingerprint of the subkey.

The current code does not anticipate signing with subkeys; in the `standalone-verify` case a signature would probably be accepted if the subkey fingerprint were specified, but for the `policy.json` case only fingerprints of the primary keys in a keyring would be accepted.

Is there an OS which creates such a setup by default?  On my Fedora 26, `gpg2` does create a subkey, but an encryption-only one, not one enabled for signing.
